title: Uragoga lacinulata
taxonomy: Uragoga lacinulata is a member of the family Annonaceae. This plant is native to tropical regions and is part of the genus Uragoga, which consists of several species of flowering plants. Uragoga lacinulata is closely related to other species within the Annonaceae family, which includes over 200 genera. The classification of Uragoga lacinulata is further divided into the order Magnoliales. The Annonaceae family is commonly known as the custard apple family.

The plant is valued for its medicinal properties and is traditionally used to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and skin infections, due to its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiseptic properties.
